evals.name: Clean a messy sales CSV with mixed issues
evals.prompt: I have a CSV file called sales_data.csv with these issues: column names like 'Order ID', 'Customer Name', 'Order Date', 'Total Amount', 'Product Category' (spaces and mixed case), about 15% missing values in Total Amount and Customer Name, 50 duplicate rows based on Order ID, and the Total Amount column is stored as strings with dollar signs like '$49.99'. The file has 10,000 rows. Write me a complete Python script using pandas to clean this data and export it as a cleaned CSV. I want a cleaning report at the end.

assertions.description: Normalizes column names to snake_case
assertions.expected: Column names are converted to snake_case (e.g., order_id, customer_name, order_date, total_amount, product_category)

assertions.description: Handles missing values with appropriate strategies
assertions.expected: Missing values in numeric columns (total_amount) use median or mean fill, and string columns (customer_name) use a placeholder like 'Unknown'

assertions.description: Deduplicates using the correct subset column
assertions.expected: Uses drop_duplicates with subset=['order_id'] or equivalent, not dropping all columns

assertions.description: Strips dollar signs before numeric conversion
assertions.expected: Removes '$' characters from total_amount before converting to numeric type using str.replace or similar

assertions.description: Uses errors='coerce' for safe type conversion
assertions.expected: Uses pd.to_numeric with errors='coerce' or equivalent safe conversion

assertions.description: Resets index after deduplication
assertions.expected: Calls reset_index(drop=True) after dropping duplicates

assertions.description: Generates a cleaning report with before/after stats
assertions.expected: Produces a summary showing original vs cleaned row counts, null counts, and duplicate counts

assertions.description: Exports cleaned data to CSV without index
assertions.expected: Uses to_csv with index=False

evals.name: Clean a Parquet file with type issues and high nulls
evals.prompt: I have a Parquet file called user_events.parquet with columns: user_id (int), event_type (string), timestamp (stored as string like '2024-01-15T10:30:00'), is_premium (string values 'true'/'false'), session_duration_seconds (object type but actually integers), page_url (string), referrer (70% null), device_type (string with 5 unique values). I need to: 1) convert timestamp to datetime, 2) convert is_premium to boolean, 3) convert session_duration_seconds to integer, 4) decide what to do with the 70% null referrer column, 5) make device_type a category. Write the cleaning code.

assertions.description: Converts timestamp string to datetime
assertions.expected: Uses pd.to_datetime() to convert the timestamp column

assertions.description: Converts is_premium to boolean type
assertions.expected: Maps 'true'/'false' strings to boolean values and converts to bool or boolean dtype

assertions.description: Converts session_duration to integer with null handling
assertions.expected: Uses pd.to_numeric or astype with Int64 (nullable) since there may be nulls

assertions.description: Addresses the 70% null referrer column appropriately
assertions.expected: Either drops the referrer column due to >50% nulls, or fills with 'Unknown'/'direct' with explicit justification

assertions.description: Converts device_type to category
assertions.expected: Converts device_type to category dtype for memory efficiency

assertions.description: Reads from Parquet format
assertions.expected: Uses pd.read_parquet() to load the file

evals.name: Handle column name collisions and complex dedup
evals.prompt: I have a CSV with these column names: 'User Name', 'user-name', 'userName', 'Email Address', 'email_address', 'Created At', 'Updated At', 'is_Active'. When I normalize these to snake_case, 'User Name', 'user-name', and 'userName' will all become 'user_name', and 'Email Address' and 'email_address' will both become 'email_address'. How should I handle these collisions? Also, I need to deduplicate but keep the row with the most recent 'Updated At' timestamp. Write the code.

assertions.description: Detects and handles column name collisions
assertions.expected: Identifies that multiple columns will map to the same snake_case name and appends suffixes like _1, _2 or otherwise disambiguates

assertions.description: Preserves all data during collision resolution
assertions.expected: Does not silently drop columns that collide; all original columns are preserved with unique names

assertions.description: Sorts by timestamp before dedup to keep latest
assertions.expected: Sorts by updated_at (or equivalent) in descending order before calling drop_duplicates with keep='first', or uses keep='last' with ascending sort

assertions.description: Converts timestamp column before sorting
assertions.expected: Converts 'Updated At'/'Created At' to datetime before using them for sort-based deduplication

assertions.description: Normalizes all column names to snake_case
assertions.expected: All final column names follow snake_case convention (lowercase, underscores)

assertions.description: Handles the is_Active column correctly
assertions.expected: Normalizes is_Active to is_active and recognizes it as a boolean column
